The subject tree is a hackberry (Celtis occidentalis) approximately 15 inches DBH located along the right-of-way adjacent to a retaining wall and beneath overhead utility lines. The tree exhibits multiple cavities in the main stem indicating internal decay and loss of structural wood. The canopy has been heavily pruned for utility clearance, resulting in an asymmetric crown and large pruning wounds. Rooting space is restricted by the adjacent retaining wall and limited planting area, which may reduce anchorage potential. Dense vine growth is also present on the trunk and canopy. These combined conditions negatively affect the tree’s structural integrity and long-term viability.Reason for Removal:Significant cavity/area of decay
		
	
	
                                
                            
                        
                    
                        
                            
                                
                                    
                                        
                                         Parcel Information

			Tree number:2Species:Chinese Privet Tree Size (DBH):7Tree location:see site mapDescription of Tree:Numbered flagging tape Reason for Removal:OtherComments:Undesired species

			Tree number:4Species:SweetgumTree Size (DBH):16Tree location:see site mapDescription of Tree:Numbered flagging tape Reason for Removal:OtherComments:Heavily covered in invasive vines. Crown in contact and rubbing with Tree 5
		
		
			Tree number:5Species:Sweetgum Tree Size (DBH):18Tree location:see site mapDescription of Tree:Numbered flagging tape Reason for Removal:OtherComments:Crown in contact and rubbing with Tree 4
		
		
			Tree number:6Species:LigustrumTree Size (DBH):6Tree location:see site mapDescription of Tree:Numbered flagging tape Reason for Removal:OtherComments:Undesired species
		
		
			Tree number:7Species:Northern Red Oakl Tree Size (DBH):32Tree location:see site mapDescription of Tree:Numbered flagging tape Reason for Removal:OtherComments:Tree appears to have initially developed as a phototropic leaner. Subsequent growth in the upper crown shows compensatory reaction growth trending more vertically. Epicormic shoots are present along the tension side consistent with this reaction growth pattern, reflecting the tree's adjustment to the earlier lean and a change in lighting conditions. While this information does not necessarily denote a defect, it does suggest a change in site conditions. A small rise in the ground surface was noticed beneath the ivy on the tension side, suggesting the presence of an old stump site within the structural root plate. The center of the presumed stump location is approximately 11 feet from the subject tree, suggesting a previously sheared or deformed structural root plate system. Soil resistance testing with a digital penetrometer (Newton measurements) indicated comparatively low soil resistance on the tension side (63.93 N and 121.7 N) near the historic stump location, while the compression side measured higher resistance (213 N and 300.7 N), indicating comparatively stronger soil support on the compression side. If the adjacent tree had remained intact and structurally sound, the subject tree’s lean alone may not present a concern; however, the historic removal, the elapsed time since that removal, and the relatively low soil resistance measured on the tension side suggest reduced soil support within that portion of the structural root plate. These soil readings prompted additional research into the site history. A large oak matching the nearby stump description was permitted for removal due to significant root decay (30-inch oak) on January 27, 2016 under permit BA-201600363. The tree is visible in Google Street View imagery from 2008 and is absent in the next available imagery by 2016, consistent with the documented removal. A Google Street View image has been included for reference.
		
		
			Tree number:8Species:LigustrumTree Size (DBH):6Tree location:see site mapDescription of Tree:Numbered flagging tape Reason for Removal:OtherComments:Undesired species
		
		
			Tree number:9Species:Hickory Tree Size (DBH):14Tree location:see site mapDescription of Tree:numbered flagging tape Reason for Removal:OtherComments:Previously topped, loss of apical dominance 
		
		
			Tree number:10Species:PineTree Size (DBH):22Tree location:see site mapDescription of Tree:numbered flagging tape Reason for Removal:OtherComments:Two fusiform cankers present. One located at a former leader union where the tree appears to have resprouted following a prior breakage event. A second fusiform canker present on the main stem high in the canopy just below the first, visible from the rear
		
		
			Tree number:11Species:Mulberry Tree Size (DBH):14Tree location:see site mapDescription of Tree:numbered flagging tape Reason for Removal:OtherComments:Undesired species. Repeated coppice regrowth
